2603.20795.title: The Anatomy of an Edit: Mechanism-Guided Activation Steering for Knowledge Editing
2603.20795.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used as knowledge bases, but keeping them up to date requires targeted knowledge editing (KE). However, it remains unclear how edits are implemented inside the model once applied. In this work, we take a mechanistic view of KE using neuron-level knowledge attribution (NLKA). Unlike prior work that focuses on pre-edit causal tracing and localization, we use post-edit attribution -- contrasting successful and failed edits -- to isolate the computations that shift when an edit succeeds. Across representative KE methods, we find a consistent pattern: mid-to-late attention predominantly promotes the new target, while attention and FFN modules cooperate to suppress the original fact. Motivated by these findings, we propose MEGA, a MEchanism-Guided Activation steering method that performs attention-residual interventions in attribution-aligned regions without modifying model weights. On CounterFact and Popular, MEGA achieves strong editing performance across KE metrics on GPT2-XL and LLaMA2-7B. Overall, our results elevate post-edit attribution from analysis to engineering signal: by pinpointing where and how edits take hold, it powers MEGA to deliver reliable, architecture-agnostic knowledge edits.
2603.20795.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）越來越多地被用作知識庫，但保持其更新需要針對性的知識編輯（KE）。然而，目前尚不清楚一旦應用編輯後，這些編輯是如何在模型內部實施的。在這項工作中，我們採取了機械性視角來看待知識編輯，使用神經元級知識歸因（NLKA）。與先前專注於編輯前因果追蹤和定位的工作不同，我們使用編輯後的歸因——對比成功和失敗的編輯——來隔離當編輯成功時所改變的計算。通過代表性的知識編輯方法，我們發現了一個一致的模式：中後期的注意力主要促進新的目標，而注意力和前饋神經網絡模塊則協同抑制原始事實。受到這些發現的啟發，我們提出了MEGA，一種機制引導激活的引導方法，該方法在與歸因對齊的區域進行注意力殘差干預，而不修改模型權重。在CounterFact和Popular上，MEGA在GPT2-XL和LLaMA2-7B的知識編輯指標上實現了強勁的編輯性能。總體而言，我們的結果將編輯後的歸因從分析提升到工程信號：通過確定編輯如何以及在哪裡生效，它驅動MEGA提供可靠的、架構無關的知識編輯。
2603.20795.author: Yuan Cao et.al.
2603.20795.authors: Yuan Cao, Mingyang Wang, Hinrich Schütze
